symptoms.symptom_name: Shooting, electric, burning pain radiating down the leg in an L5 dermatomal pattern

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dermatomal shooting, electric, burning quality is characteristic of nerve-root irritation. In a patient with prior disc herniation this strongly supports a radicular flare.
A shooting nerve-root pattern rather than a diffuse calf ache argues against DVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute 48-hour exacerbation on a background of chronic low back pain after prior disc herniation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A prior disc herniation predisposes to recurrent nerve-root compression and radicular flares.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe pain on weight-bearing causing functional immobility (temporarily bedbound)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe pain-limited immobility reflects flare severity but is non-specific; importantly it drives the falls/access risk rather than distinguishing diagnoses.
Preserved bladder/bowel function on targeted history substantially lowers cauda equina probability, though it does not fully exclude an evolving CES and mandates safety-netting.
Absence of saddle sensory change on targeted questioning is a strong negative for CES. Definitive exclusion requires perianal examination, which is not possible via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No new or progressive leg weakness — movement preserved against resistance, limited by pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute complete disc extrusion with progressive neurological deficit
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a new or progressive motor deficit argues against a large extrusion or evolving CES requiring urgent surgery.

symptoms.symptom_name: No unilateral calf swelling, redness, or warmth

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of unilateral calf swelling, redness, or warmth lowers DVT probability, consistent with a Wells low-probability picture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Straight leg raise positive at 30 degrees on the affected side (in-person examination finding)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive straight leg raise at a low angle indicates nerve-root tension and supports lumbar radiculopathy. Cannot be reliably reproduced or verified via telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hyperaesthesia over the L5 dermatome (in-person examination finding)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dermatomal hyperaesthesia localises the irritated nerve root and supports L5 radiculopathy. Requires clinician sensory examination; not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact perianal sensation and no saddle anaesthesia on examination; motor power globally 5/5 with symmetrical reflexes (in-person examination finding)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact perianal sensation, normal power, and symmetrical reflexes on formal examination argue strongly against cauda equina and progressive deficit. These are clinician-only findings sealed from the AI-Doctor-readable layer.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of intact continence, no saddle sensory change, and no progressive weakness on targeted history constitutes a negative cauda equina screen. Together they lower CES probability enough to support a non-emergency pathway, provided robust safety-netting is given because history cannot fully substitute for examination.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person neurological and perianal examination (including anal tone and saddle sensation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive exclusion of cauda equina requires clinician examination that telehealth cannot provide. Prompt in-person review is the appropriate pathway when history red flags are negative but severe.

Indicated to characterise nerve-root compression and guide management given a severe flare on a background of prior disc herniation; becomes emergent (same-day) if any cauda equina feature emerges. Cannot be arranged via telehealth alone.
If there is any doubt about urinary retention, an objective bladder scan supports or refutes early cauda equina; not obtainable remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate lumbar spine X-ray as first-line
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Plain radiography is not first-line for radicular pain and does not exclude cauda equina or characterise disc/nerve-root pathology; recommending it as the primary investigation is inappropriate over-investigation in this acute context.
